name: Easter demand uplift
rationale: People order bigger boxes before the Easter long weekend. Dave always ordered 30-40% extra across all items.

name: Christmas week surge
rationale: Two weeks before Christmas, order volumes spike 40-60%. Suppliers also slow down so need to order earlier.

name: Perishable bruising buffer
rationale: Roughly 1-2% of perishable items arrive damaged or expire before delivery during warmer months (Oct-Apr in Australia). Dave always padded perishable orders in summer.

name: Hot weather demand drop
rationale: In extreme heat (>35C), people eat lighter and order fewer heavy protein boxes. Dave would trim orders by ~10%.

name: New item safety buffer
rationale: New products have unpredictable demand. Dave always kept 50% extra safety stock for the first month.

name: Payday demand bump
rationale: Wages are paid fortnightly on the 15th and end-of-month. Dave noticed a ~10% order bump around paydays.

name: Zero demand suppression
rationale: Items where last week's actual sales were below 10 units are likely intermittent or in steep demand decline. The ML model lags reality in these cases — its 4-week rolling features still reflect historical high demand. Cap the forecast at 1.5× last week's actuals to prevent large over-orders for slow-moving items (e.g. item selling 7 units/week gets a max forecast of ~10, not 138).
